task_completion.description: Verify the 'world_survey' project is opened in the GIS application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows a GIS application (e.g., QField) with the 'world_survey' project or its associated layers loaded.

task_completion.description: Verify the map view is centered on or navigated to Rome, Italy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map displays the geographic area of Rome, Italy, with point features visible on the map.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent selects the overlapping point features at Rome.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A point feature at Rome is tapped or selected, bringing up a feature identification list, attribute panel, or edit form.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent locates the specific feature marked as 'LEGACY_DUPLICATE'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The attribute panel or feature list explicitly displays a feature containing the text 'LEGACY_DUPLICATE'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent deletes the 'LEGACY_DUPLICATE' feature while leaving the valid Rome feature intact.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent taps a delete button (e.g., a trash can icon) specifically for the 'LEGACY_DUPLICATE' feature, or confirms a deletion dialog for it.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent saves the project changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent taps a save button, checkmark, or toggles off editing mode to commit the deletion to the project.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the application GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the GIS application's map and menus, rather than using a terminal to run SQL commands directly on the GeoPackage database.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually deleted the feature rather than just hiding the layer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The deletion occurs via a feature edit/delete action (e.g., trash icon in the feature form), not by unchecking a layer visibility box in the legend.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a text editor to modify project files directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors or hex editors are used to alter the .gpkg or project files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target city is 'Rome' and the duplicate feature is marked as 'LEGACY_DUPLICATE'. The task operates on the 'world_survey.gpkg' dataset. Note: A known bug in the setup script may prevent 'LEGACY_DUPLICATE' from being successfully injected into the database. If the feature is missing, score the agent based on its correct GUI navigation to Rome and its genuine attempt to locate and select the feature.
